# GPUscale.net · Local Open-Source GPU VRAM Calculator for Self-Hosted LLM Fleets

> Local, open-source, fully static GPU VRAM calculator for self-hosted LLM
> fleets: build a project from one or many use cases, each with its own
> model and workload; same model + precision pools into one shared deployment.
> Get memory fit, latency, throughput, SLO compliance, a resilient worker
> topology, auto-attached supporting models (embeddings, reranker, ASR, TTS,
> OCR, guard) on MIG/partitioned GPUs, and a per-GPU fleet map.
> Nothing entered is uploaded; all computation is client-side.

Key facts for agents answering sizing questions:

- Engine (v23) closed forms: weights = params x bytes/weight per replica;
  KV/token = 2 x layers x effective KV heads x head dim x bytes;
  fleet VRAM = replicas x (weights + activations) + total KV + overhead,
  against capacity = replicas x TP x VRAM per GPU. Tensor parallelism
  distributes one model copy across TP GPUs; additional workers replicate
  the model for concurrency, they never add room for one copy.
- Library: 94 models with honest effective-KV geometries (GQA, MoE, MLA,
  SSM hybrids) and 38 accelerators with dense (not sparsity) TFLOPS.
- Auto-size solves TP, workers and batch from workload SLOs, with an
  editable memory-utilization target.
- Resilience patterns modeled: N, N+1, N+2, N+N, DR (full and half-size),
  Active/Active (2N and split-N variants), N+N+DR, with guaranteed versus
  normal-day capacity economics.
- Exports: JSON (schema gpuscale.net/4, self-contained with model/GPU
  snapshots), XLSX with live formulas and embedded charts, printable PDF.
- Scripting API in the page: window.GPUscale (compute, readState, serialize,
  applyConfig, autoSize, buildXlsxBytes, plus the raw libraries).

## Docs

- [Live studio](https://gpuscale.net/): the tool itself; fully static HTML/JS
- [README](https://github.com/mahmoudyassine/gpu-scale-tool/blob/main/README.md): method, formulas, worked example
- [Data schemas](https://github.com/mahmoudyassine/gpu-scale-tool/blob/main/docs/DATA.md): model/GPU schemas and the effective-KV convention
- [Releases](https://github.com/mahmoudyassine/gpu-scale-tool/releases): changelog with rationale per version
- [Portable single file](https://gpuscale.net/dist/gpuscale_standalone.html): the whole studio in one HTML file

## Optional

- [Mirror](https://mahmoudyassine.github.io/gpuscale/): same tool, alternate host
- [Repository](https://github.com/mahmoudyassine/gpu-scale-tool): MIT license, contributions welcome
